hero.description: Answers to the most frequently asked questions about Harch Corp, our infrastructure, and operations.
hero.label: FAQ
hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions

q1.answer: Harch Corp is a sovereign infrastructure conglomerate based in Casablanca, Morocco. We operate across 8 vertical subsidiaries covering AI compute, renewable energy, cement production, desalination, mining, agriculture, finance, and technology.
q1.question: What is Harch Corp?

q10.answer: Visit our Careers page to view open positions or submit a spontaneous application. Our hiring process is designed to be fast and transparent — 6 steps, 2 weeks, zero bureaucracy.
q10.question: How can I apply to work at Harch Corp?

q11.answer: We offer a range of NVIDIA GPUs including H100, A100, and L40S series. All are powered by renewable energy and available on-demand, reserved, or at spot pricing.
q11.question: What GPU types are available?

q12.answer: Yes, our Sovereign plan offers classified infrastructure for government agencies and defense organizations, with sovereign data hosting, dedicated security operations center, and air-gap architecture available.
q12.question: Do you offer classified government solutions?

q2.answer: Sovereign means our systems are designed with data sovereignty and operational autonomy as foundational principles. Data is hosted locally in Morocco, communications are end-to-end encrypted, and we depend on no foreign infrastructure.
q2.question: What does 'sovereign' mean?

q3.answer: All data is hosted exclusively in Morocco on infrastructure we control. No cross-border data transfer, no dependency on foreign cloud providers.
q3.question: Where is data hosted?

q4.answer: Our GPU cloud is powered by Moroccan renewable energy at ~47 gCO2/kWh — among the lowest in the world. Our orchestrator automatically shifts workloads to the cleanest periods and regions, reducing carbon footprint by up to 40%.
q4.question: What is carbon-aware GPU cloud?

q5.answer: We currently operate in Morocco with planned deployments in Gambia, Senegal, Mauritania, and Algeria. Our goal is to expand operations to 5+ countries by 2030.
q5.question: Which countries do you operate in?

q6.answer: Harch Corp works with qualified institutional investors. Please visit our Investor Relations page to learn more or request an investor briefing.
q6.question: How can I invest in Harch Corp?

q7.answer: We are GDPR-compliant, ISO 27001 certified, and SOC 2 Type II certified. All communications are encrypted with AES-256 and our security operations run 24/7/365.
q7.question: What security certifications do you have?

q8.answer: We welcome technology, financial, industrial, and government partnerships. Please visit our Partners page to learn more or submit a partner status application.
q8.question: How do I become a partner?

q9.answer: HarchOS is our unified operating system that manages the entire infrastructure portfolio. It uses a SENSE-THINK-ACT architecture for real-time monitoring, AI-powered analysis, and automated execution across all subsidiaries.
q9.question: What is HarchOS?

hero.description: Key terms and definitions for understanding HarchOS and sovereign infrastructure.
hero.label: Glossary
hero.title: Glossary

glossary.noResultsDescription: Try a different search term or browse all terms.

aiInference.definition: The process of using a trained AI model to make predictions on new data. HarchOS provides optimized inference endpoints with sub-50ms latency.

airGapped.definition: A security measure where systems are physically isolated from unsecured networks. HarchOS supports air-gapped deployments for classified government workloads.

api.definition: Application Programming Interface — a set of protocols for building and integrating software. HarchOS provides REST, gRPC, and WebSocket APIs.

carbonAwareComputing.definition: Computing that adapts workload scheduling based on the carbon intensity of the energy grid. HarchOS implements real-time carbon-aware scheduling across all hubs.

carbonIntensity.definition: The amount of CO2 emitted per kilowatt-hour of electricity generated. HarchOS achieves ~47 gCO2/kWh — 89% below the global average.

cloudComputing.definition: On-demand delivery of compute resources over the internet. HarchOS provides sovereign cloud computing with guaranteed African data residency.

compute.definition: Processing power provided by CPUs or GPUs for running workloads. HarchOS offers GPU compute with H100, A100, and L40S accelerators.

containerization.definition: Packaging applications with their dependencies into portable containers. HarchOS supports containerized workloads with live migration across hubs.

dataCenter.definition: A facility housing computer systems and associated components. HarchOS operates 5 sovereign data centers across Morocco.

dataSovereignty.definition: The principle that data is subject to the laws of the country where it is stored. HarchOS guarantees 100% African data sovereignty with Morocco jurisdiction.

desalination.definition: The process of removing salt from seawater to produce fresh water. Harch Water operates AI-optimized desalination plants across Morocco.

dpa.definition: Data Processing Agreement — a legal contract between data controllers and processors. Required for GDPR compliance in all HarchOS deployments.

edgeComputing.definition: Processing data closer to where it is generated rather than in a centralized data center. HarchOS supports edge computing for IoT and industrial workloads.

egress.definition: Data transferred out of a network or cloud service. HarchOS offers reduced egress fees with sovereign data residency guarantees.

esg.definition: Environmental, Social, and Governance — a framework for evaluating corporate sustainability. Harch Corp integrates ESG across all subsidiaries.

fogComputing.definition: An extension of cloud computing that brings processing closer to data sources. HarchOS uses fog computing for real-time industrial monitoring.

freeTier.definition: A no-cost usage tier with limited resources. HarchOS offers a free tier with 10 GPU-hours per month for development and testing.

gpu.definition: Graphics Processing Unit — specialized hardware for parallel computing. HarchOS provides NVIDIA H100, A100, and L40S GPUs for AI workloads.

greenComputing.definition: Environmentally sustainable computing practices. HarchOS implements green computing through carbon-aware scheduling and renewable energy.

greenHydrogen.definition: Hydrogen produced using renewable energy through electrolysis. Harch Energy is developing green hydrogen projects across Morocco.

grpc.definition: A high-performance RPC framework using Protocol Buffers. HarchOS provides gRPC services for low-latency inter-service communication.

harchOS.definition: The operating system for sovereign infrastructure — HarchOS unifies monitoring, decision-making, and execution across 8 subsidiaries using the SENSE-THINK-ACT paradigm.

hyperscale.definition: Massive-scale cloud computing infrastructure. HarchOS is designed for hyperscale deployment with multi-hub orchestration.

iaas.definition: Infrastructure as a Service — cloud computing that provides virtualized computing resources. HarchOS offers sovereign IaaS with African data residency.

inference.definition: The process of running predictions through a trained model. HarchOS provides optimized inference with sub-50ms p99 latency.

islamicFinance.definition: Financial services compliant with Islamic law (Sharia). Harch Finance offers Sharia-compliant investment products including Sukuk.

iso27001.definition: An international standard for information security management. HarchOS is ISO 27001 certified across all data centers.

kubernetes.definition: An open-source container orchestration platform. HarchOS integrates with Kubernetes for workload management and auto-scaling.

latency.definition: The time delay between a request and response. HarchOS achieves <100ms intra-Morocco latency and <10ms between hubs.

llm.definition: Large Language Model — AI models trained on vast text datasets. HarchOS provides optimized hosting for sovereign LLM deployment.

loadBalancer.definition: A device that distributes network traffic across multiple servers. HarchOS uses intelligent load balancing with carbon-aware routing.

microservices.definition: An architectural style where applications are composed of small, independent services. HarchOS is built on a microservices architecture.

multiTenant.definition: A software architecture where multiple customers share the same infrastructure. HarchOS provides secure multi-tenancy with resource isolation.

onPremises.definition: Software or infrastructure installed and run on the user's premises. HarchOS supports on-premises deployment for classified environments.

openapi.definition: A specification for machine-readable interface files for REST APIs. HarchOS APIs are documented using the OpenAPI 3.0 specification.

paas.definition: Platform as a Service — cloud computing that provides a platform for developing and deploying applications. HarchOS offers sovereign PaaS with carbon-aware optimization.

pemElectrolysis.definition: A method of producing hydrogen using a solid polymer electrolyte. Harch Energy uses PEM electrolysis for green hydrogen production.

phosphateMining.definition: The extraction of phosphate minerals for use in fertilizers and industrial applications. Morocco holds 75% of the world's phosphate reserves.

precisionAgriculture.definition: Farming management using IoT, AI, and satellite data to optimize crop yields. Harch Agri provides precision agriculture across West Africa.

pue.definition: Power Usage Effectiveness — a metric for data center energy efficiency. HarchOS achieves a PUE of 1.04 at its best hub.

rbac.definition: Role-Based Access Control — a method of restricting system access based on user roles. HarchOS implements granular RBAC across all services.

renewableEnergy.definition: Energy from sources that are naturally replenishing. HarchOS data centers are powered by 81.5% renewable energy on average.

restApi.definition: Representational State Transfer API — an architectural style for web services. HarchOS provides a comprehensive REST API for all platform operations.

saas.definition: Software as a Service — cloud-based software delivery model. HarchOS offers SaaS solutions for infrastructure management and monitoring.

senseThinkAct.definition: The HarchOS architectural paradigm — SENSE collects data, THINK analyzes and decides, ACT executes operations autonomously.

soc2.definition: Service Organization Control 2 — a compliance framework for service providers. HarchOS is SOC 2 Type II certified.

sovereignAi.definition: AI systems that operate within a country's legal jurisdiction with guaranteed data sovereignty. HarchOS provides sovereign AI with Morocco data residency.

sovereignCloud.definition: Cloud infrastructure that operates within a specific jurisdiction with guaranteed data residency. HarchOS is Africa's first sovereign GPU cloud.

submarineCable.definition: Undersea fiber-optic cables that carry data between continents. Morocco connects to Europe via 4 submarine cable systems.

sukuk.definition: Islamic financial certificates similar to bonds but compliant with Sharia law. Harch Finance offers Sukuk-based investment products.

terraform.definition: An infrastructure as code tool for provisioning and managing cloud resources. HarchOS provides Terraform providers and modules for sovereign deployments.

throughput.definition: The amount of data processed in a given time period. HarchOS achieves 10TB/day log ingestion and 1M+ data points per second.

verticalFarming.definition: Growing crops in vertically stacked layers using controlled environments. Harch Agri is developing vertical farming solutions for urban Africa.

verticalIntegration.definition: A business strategy where a company controls its entire supply chain. Harch Corp's vertical integration spans from raw materials to finished products.

virtualization.definition: Creating virtual versions of computing resources. HarchOS uses hardware virtualization for GPU allocation and multi-tenant isolation.

waterDesalination.definition: The process of removing salt and minerals from seawater. Harch Water operates AI-optimized desalination plants across Morocco.

webhook.definition: An HTTP callback triggered by specific events. HarchOS provides webhooks for real-time event notifications and integrations.

competitive.title: Competitive Landscape
competitive.subtitle: HarchOS vs. the world's GPU infrastructure providers. We don't just run green — we schedule green. No competitor matches us on a single metric.

coreweave.name: CoreWeave
coreweave.verdict: CoreWeave has more GPUs. HarchOS has everything else — 9.5x lower carbon intensity, 4x cheaper energy, 5-nines SLA, live container migration, real-time carbon-aware scheduling, African sovereignty, and submarine cable gateway to Europe. Scale without sovereignty is just rented infrastructure.

googleCloud.name: Google Cloud (Hamina)
googleCloud.verdict: Google Hamina is a green data center you cannot use for GPU compute. HarchOS is a green GPU cloud you can — at 3x lower energy cost, with African sovereignty, real-time carbon-aware scheduling, and the only submarine cable gateway between Africa and Europe. Access matters.
googleCloud.m5Competitor: N/A — no GPU cloud to tax
googleCloud.m9Competitor: GDPR only — US CLOUD Act applies

africaDataCentres.name: Africa Data Centres (Cassava)
africaDataCentres.verdict: Africa's largest DC operator has zero GPUs, zero carbon-awareness, zero AI platform, 17x less power pipeline, 10x higher carbon intensity, and 3x higher energy costs. HarchOS is not competing with Africa Data Centres — we are in a different category entirely.
africaDataCentres.m9Competitor: South Africa — US CLOUD Act via partners

qscale.name: QScale
qscale.verdict: QScale runs on a clean grid with lower grid carbon, but cannot schedule carbon per-job, cannot serve GPU cloud workloads, cannot reach Europe in under 80ms, cannot reach Africa at all, and cannot offer sovereignty. HarchOS is the only platform that combines carbon-awareness + GPU cloud + EU proximity + African sovereignty + industrial integration.

lambdaLabs.name: Lambda Labs
lambdaLabs.verdict: Lambda Labs offers cheap GPU access on a fossil-fueled US grid. HarchOS offers sovereign GPU compute at 4x lower energy cost, 10x lower carbon, 5-nines SLA, cross-hub live migration, and African jurisdiction. Cheap is not the same as superior.

oracleCloud.name: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
oracleCloud.verdict: Oracle has one African region on a fossil-fueled grid, with no carbon-awareness, no sovereign guarantees, and US CLOUD Act exposure. HarchOS has 5 hubs on 81.5% renewable energy, 100% African sovereignty, carbon-aware scheduling, and 4x lower energy costs. Sovereignty is not a feature — it is the architecture.

equinix.name: Equinix
equinix.verdict: Equinix buys RECs to claim 95% renewable — but RECs do not reduce actual carbon. HarchOS runs on 81.5% direct renewable generation at 47 gCO2/kWh — no accounting tricks, no offsets, just clean power from our own solar and wind farms. Direct beats purchased. Always.

intelligence.name: Harch Intelligence
intelligence.heroTitle:
Africa's Largest AI
Hyperscale Data Center
intelligence.heroSubtitle: 1,798 carbon-optimized GPUs across 5 hubs — sovereign AI compute infrastructure powered by Africa's lowest-carbon electricity
intelligence.overview: Harch Intelligence operates 1,798 carbon-optimized GPUs across 5 hubs in Morocco — powered primarily by renewable energy and designed to serve as the backbone of Africa's sovereign AI compute infrastructure. With carbon-aware scheduling as our #1 differentiator, we route workloads to the greenest hubs in real time, achieving an average carbon intensity of ~47 gCO2/kWh — 89% below the industry average. Our 500MW Pipeline expands capacity at Dakhla with direct submarine cable connectivity to Europe and the Americas. This is not merely a data center — it is the foundation of Africa's digital sovereignty, ensuring that the continent's data, compute, and AI capabilities remain under African control.
intelligence.strategicContext: The global AI infrastructure race is accelerating, with over $300 billion invested in data center capacity in 2024 alone. Yet Africa, home to 1.4 billion people and the world's fastest-growing digital economy, hosts less than 1% of global data center capacity. This structural deficit forces African enterprises, governments, and researchers to rely on foreign infrastructure — creating dependencies that compromise data sovereignty, increase latency, and extract capital from the continent. Harch Intelligence directly addresses this imbalance by building sovereign AI compute capacity at the scale the continent demands. Our 5 hubs across Morocco deliver 1,798 GPUs with carbon-aware scheduling, and our 500MW Pipeline in Dakhla positions Morocco as the gateway for AI compute between Europe and Africa, leveraging the country's exceptional renewable energy resources, strategic Atlantic coastline with submarine cable landing stations, and political stability.
intelligence.marketAnalysis: Africa's cloud and data center market is projected to reach $15 billion by 2028, growing at 25% CAGR — the fastest growth rate globally. Key demand drivers include government data localization mandates sweeping across Nigeria, Kenya, South Africa, and Egypt; the explosive growth of African fintech processing over $500 billion annually; the expansion of AI-driven healthcare diagnostics serving 300 million patients; and the rise of sovereign AI initiatives from the African Union and regional bodies. Harch Intelligence's 1,798 GPUs across 5 hubs capture a significant share of this demand while operating at cost structures 40-60% cheaper than AWS/GCP/Azure, thanks to Dakhla's renewable energy costs of $0.03/kWh — among the lowest on the planet. Our carbon-aware scheduling achieves ~47 gCO2/kWh average carbon intensity, 89% below the industry average of ~450 gCO2/kWh.
intelligence.sustainability: Every aspect of Harch Intelligence is designed for minimal environmental impact and maximum sustainability. The facility runs on 100% renewable energy from Harch Energy's solar and wind installations, eliminating scope 2 emissions entirely. Our hybrid liquid-air cooling system achieves a PUE below 1.15 — among the most efficient in the global industry — while using seawater for heat rejection to minimize freshwater consumption. Waste heat from GPU clusters is captured and directed to adjacent agricultural greenhouses, creating a circular energy model. The facility's construction uses 40% recycled steel and low-carbon concrete from Harch Cement, while all electronic waste follows certified recycling protocols. We target LEED Platinum and BREEAM Outstanding certifications.

capabilities.title: Carbon-Aware Data Centers
capabilities.desc: 5 GPU hubs across Morocco with carbon-aware scheduling as our #1 differentiator. Our 500MW Pipeline in Dakhla is designed for the most demanding compute workloads, with modular architecture allowing capacity to scale from 100MW to 500MW in phases. Each hub features redundant power paths, diverse fiber entrances, and N+1 cooling redundancy throughout. Every rack is pre-wired for liquid cooling, enabling deployment of next-generation GPUs without retrofitting.

capabilities.title: GPU Clusters
capabilities.desc: 1,798 GPUs across 5 hubs optimized for training and inference with carbon-aware scheduling. Harch Ouarzazate (800 GPUs, 97.2% renewable, 18 gCO2/kWh), Harch Dakhla (400 GPUs, 94.8% renewable, 32 gCO2/kWh), Harch Benguerir (350 GPUs, 88.5% renewable, 55 gCO2/kWh), Harch Tanger (200 GPUs, 82.1% renewable, 95 gCO2/kWh), Harch Casablanca (48 GPUs, 45% renewable, 210 gCO2/kWh). Our largest hub supports distributed training across 800-GPU partitions at Ouarzazate, enabling training of large-scale models with industry-leading carbon efficiency.

capabilities.title: Renewable Energy Integration
capabilities.desc: 81.5% average renewable energy across all hubs, powered by Harch Energy's solar and wind infrastructure. Carbon-aware scheduling routes workloads to the greenest hub in real time, achieving ~47 gCO2/kWh average — 89% below the industry average of ~450 gCO2/kWh. Zero-carbon electricity at Ouarzazate and Dakhla delivers cost advantages of 40-60% cheaper than AWS/GCP/Azure. On-site battery storage provides backup for uninterrupted operations during grid transitions.

capabilities.title: Submarine Connectivity
capabilities.desc: Direct connection to 4 submarine cable systems — including the Africa Coast to Europe (ACE), MainOne, and Maroc Telecom cables — providing ultra-low latency to Europe (8ms), the Americas (35ms), and the Middle East (20ms). Dual diverse landing stations ensure path redundancy for all international traffic.

capabilities.title: Sovereign Security
capabilities.desc: End-to-end security from physical access to data encryption. The facility operates under a sovereign security framework with armed perimeter security, biometric access controls, and 24/7 surveillance. Harch Technology's cybersecurity suite provides sovereign-grade protection including DDoS mitigation, intrusion detection, and encrypted data pathways for sensitive compute workloads.

capabilities.title: AI Platform Services
capabilities.desc: Full-stack AI platform from data ingestion to model deployment. Managed services for training, fine-tuning, and inference with enterprise SLAs guaranteeing 99.99% availability. The platform supports PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX natively, with pre-built MLOps pipelines for automated model lifecycle management.

cement.name: Harch Cement
cement.heroSubtitle: 500kT/yr cement production serving the construction boom with vertically integrated operations
cement.overview: Harch Cement is developing a 500kT/yr cement production facility in Gambia, serving West Africa's construction boom with vertically integrated operations from quarry to delivery. Our model captures the full value chain — from limestone extraction through clinker production to finished cement distribution — creating structural cost advantages of 30-50% versus import-dependent competitors. This is not simply a cement plant; it is an industrial anchor that catalyzes infrastructure development, creates hundreds of direct jobs, and eliminates West Africa's dependence on imported construction materials.
cement.strategicContext: West Africa faces a fundamental construction materials deficit. The region imports over 15 million tonnes of cement annually, paying premium prices that inflate infrastructure costs by 40-70% compared to markets with domestic production. Gambia, with a population of 2.5 million and a GDP growing at 6% annually, currently imports 100% of its cement — a structural vulnerability that increases with every infrastructure project. Harch Cement's 500kT/yr facility captures a significant share of this demand while building domestic industrial capacity that strengthens economic sovereignty.
cement.marketAnalysis: The West African cement market is valued at $8.5 billion and growing at 8% CAGR, driven by urbanization, government infrastructure programs totaling $45 billion across ECOWAS nations, and a housing deficit exceeding 50 million units. Gambia alone imports 400,000 tonnes of cement annually at premium prices averaging $120/tonne versus $65-75/tonne for domestically produced cement.
cement.sustainability: Harch Cement integrates sustainability at every level of operations. Our green cement formulations use locally sourced pozzolanic materials to reduce the clinker factor below 85%, cutting CO2 emissions by 25% versus ordinary Portland cement. The kiln incorporates waste heat recovery technology that captures 30% of thermal energy for power generation, reducing grid electricity consumption. Quarry rehabilitation plans are developed before extraction begins, with progressive restoration using overburden and topsoil stockpiles.

capabilities.title: Quarry Operations
capabilities.desc: Vertically integrated limestone quarry with 50+ year verified reserves. In-country raw material sourcing eliminates import dependency and reduces raw material costs by 30%.

capabilities.title: Modern Kiln Technology
capabilities.desc: State-of-the-art rotary kiln with waste heat recovery and AI-optimized production control. The 5-stage preheater with calciner achieves 40% lower energy consumption versus regional competitors.

capabilities.title: Green Cement Innovation
capabilities.desc: Blended cement formulations using locally sourced pozzolanic and slag materials, reducing the clinker factor to below 85% and the carbon footprint by 25%. Our R&D team is developing calcined clay (LC3) formulations targeting clinker factors below 70% by 2029.

capabilities.title: Regional Distribution Network
capabilities.desc: Strategic location on the Gambia River with barge and road access to Senegal, Guinea-Bissau, and Guinea. 500km distribution radius served by a fleet of 40 cement tankers and river barges.

capabilities.title: Quality Assurance Systems
capabilities.desc: ISO 9001 certified production with AI-powered quality monitoring and automated sampling at every production stage. Consistent product quality that exceeds both EN 197 and ASTM C150 standards.

capabilities.title: Market Position & Strategy
capabilities.desc: First-mover advantage in Gambia with significant barriers to entry for competitors. 60% domestic market share target within 3 years of commissioning.

energy.name: Harch Energy
energy.heroSubtitle: Solar, wind, and green hydrogen powering industrial sovereignty across the continent
energy.overview: Harch Energy is developing over 2GW+ Pipeline of renewable energy capacity across Morocco and the Sahel region — combining solar, wind, and green hydrogen production to power industrial operations and data centers with zero-carbon electricity. Our integrated approach ensures energy sovereignty for the continent while creating a model for sustainable industrialization worldwide.
energy.strategicContext: Africa possesses the world's greatest renewable energy potential — 40% of global solar irradiance and exceptional wind corridors across the Sahel. Yet the continent generates only 3% of global renewable electricity, and over 600 million Africans lack access to reliable power. Harch Energy captures this opportunity at industrial scale, building the renewable infrastructure that powers not just Harch Corp's verticals but catalyzes a continental energy transformation.
energy.marketAnalysis: Africa's renewable energy market is projected to reach $80 billion in annual investment by 2030, driven by falling technology costs (solar PV costs have declined 90% since 2010), international climate finance commitments exceeding $100 billion annually, and rapidly growing power demand. Morocco alone plans 6GW of new renewable capacity by 2030.
energy.sustainability: Harch Energy's entire business model is predicated on sustainability. Every megawatt we generate displaces fossil fuel generation, preventing approximately 1,000 tonnes of CO2 emissions annually. Our 2GW+ Pipeline will offset over 3.2 million tonnes of CO2 per year — equivalent to removing 700,000 cars from the road.

capabilities.title: Solar Photovoltaic
capabilities.desc: 1.2GW of solar photovoltaic capacity across Morocco's southern regions using Tier-1 bifacial panels with single-axis trackers for maximum energy yield. Capacity factors of 28% — significantly above the global average.

capabilities.title: Onshore Wind Power
capabilities.desc: 800MW of onshore wind capacity in the Sahel corridor, one of the world's premier wind resources. Capacity factors above 45%, generating electricity at $18/MWh — competitive with any generation source globally.

capabilities.title: Green Hydrogen Production
capabilities.desc: 200MW electrolyzer capacity for green hydrogen production using PEM technology. Production cost targeted at $2.50/kg by 2028 — competitive with grey hydrogen in European markets.

capabilities.title: Grid Integration & Storage
capabilities.desc: AI-optimized grid management with 400MWh of battery storage ensuring stable power supply 24/7 for critical industrial loads.

mining.strategicContext: The global energy transition is creating unprecedented demand for critical minerals. Africa holds world-class reserves of all these minerals, yet remains primarily a raw material exporter.
mining.marketAnalysis: The global critical minerals market is valued at $320 billion and growing at 12% CAGR. Harch Mining's strategy of in-country processing captures 5-8x more value per tonne versus raw ore export.

ontology.label: DATA MODEL
ontology.name: Ontology
ontology.description: A unified semantic model that represents every asset, contract, sensor, and operational entity across Harch Corp's eight verticals. The Ontology eliminates data silos and establishes a single source of truth — enabling operators, analysts, and decision-makers to work from the same operational picture, whether they manage a cement plant in Casablanca or a desalination facility in Dakar.

0.title: Unified Object Model
0.description: Every physical asset, legal entity, and operational process modeled as a first-class object with typed properties, provenance, and lineage — from turbines to transport contracts.

1.title: Cross-Vertical Relationship Mapping
1.description: Automatic detection and visualization of dependencies between verticals: how a water shortage impacts cement production, how energy pricing shifts mining output, how logistics bottlenecks cascade across operations.

2.title: Real-Time Data Synchronization
2.description: Bidirectional sync with ERP systems (SAP, Oracle), SCADA networks, IoT sensor arrays, and legacy databases — ensuring the Ontology reflects operational reality, not stale reports.

3.title: Temporal Versioning
3.description: Every object maintains a full history of state changes, enabling retrospective analysis, audit compliance, and point-in-time reconstruction of any operational decision.

foundry.label: DATA INTEGRATION
foundry.name: Foundry
foundry.description: The data integration layer that connects, transforms, and pipelines information from every source Harch Corp operates. Foundry aggregates data from enterprise resource planning systems, legacy databases, SCADA networks, and operational sources to identify bottlenecks, streamline engineering workflows, and support proactive risk mitigation — ultimately providing a unified, data-driven approach to infrastructure management.

0.title: Demand Planning & Procurement Optimization
0.description: Aggregate demand signals across verticals and optimize procurement timelines against supplier capacity, shipping constraints, and storage costs. Reduce overstocking and stockouts through forward-looking pipeline visibility.

1.title: Dynamic Production Scheduling
1.description: Automatically adjust production schedules based on real-time input: equipment availability, energy costs, labor constraints, and downstream demand shifts. Recalibrate in minutes, not days.

2.title: Process Optimization & Non-Compliance Reporting
2.description: Detect process deviations against regulatory and internal thresholds in real time. Generate structured non-compliance reports with root-cause analysis and corrective action recommendations.

3.title: Multi-Source Pipeline Orchestration
3.description: Build, monitor, and audit data pipelines that move information between SAP, Oracle, SQL Server, S3, IoT gateways, and flat files — with full data lineage and quality checks at every stage.

aip.label: ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
aip.name: AIP
aip.description: The AI platform layer that enables operators, engineers, and executives to interact with infrastructure data through natural language, build AI-driven workflows, and deploy intelligent automation across operations. AIP goes beyond chat: it builds AI applications, action-driven logic, and production automations that run on operational data — not training datasets.

0.title: AI-Driven Workflow Builder
0.description: Design, test, and deploy AI-powered workflows that combine large language models with operational data. Build automations that monitor conditions, evaluate options, and propose actions for human review — not black-box decisions.

1.title: Action-Driven Logic
1.description: Define business rules and decision logic that trigger real operational actions: reallocate materials, adjust production parameters, escalate maintenance tickets, or reroute logistics — all with full audit trails and human-in-the-loop controls.

2.title: Scenario Simulation & Stress Testing
2.description: Run Monte Carlo simulations and stress tests against real operational constraints: what happens to cement output if energy prices rise 15%? What if the Tangier port shuts for 48 hours? Quantified answers, not guesses.

3.title: Predictive Maintenance & Anomaly Detection
3.description: Pattern recognition across sensor data, maintenance logs, and operational telemetry to identify equipment failures before they occur. Reduce unplanned downtime and extend asset lifespan with evidence-based maintenance scheduling.

apollo.label: DEPLOYMENT & OPERATIONS
apollo.name: Apollo
apollo.description: The deployment and operations engine that manages HarchOS software across production environments — from data centers in Casablanca to edge nodes in remote mining sites. Apollo handles versioning, rollout strategy, health monitoring, and rollback, ensuring that every update to HarchOS reaches every site safely and without downtime.

0.title: Zero-Downtime Continuous Deployment
0.description: Deploy HarchOS updates across all production environments with canary strategies, blue-green rollouts, and automatic health gates. Every deployment is reversible, every change is tracked.

1.title: Multi-Region Operations Management
1.description: Monitor and manage HarchOS instances across Morocco, Senegal, Ivory Coast, and emerging markets from a single control plane. Regional isolation with centralized visibility.

2.title: Automated Rollback & Incident Response
2.description: When a deployment fails health checks, Apollo automatically reverts to the last stable version and generates an incident report with root-cause analysis. Mean time to recovery measured in seconds, not hours.

3.title: Infrastructure Access Control
3.description: Granular, role-based access to production environments with full audit logging. No shared credentials, no untracked SSH sessions, no exceptions.

build.label: INFRASTRUCTURE CONSTRUCTION
build.name: Build
build.description: The construction management platform for Harch Corp's infrastructure pipeline. Build aggregates data from project management systems, site surveys, contractor databases, and financial models to identify schedule risks, optimize resource allocation, and support proactive risk mitigation — providing a unified, data-driven approach to construction project delivery from feasibility to commissioning.

0.title: Demand Planning & Resource Optimization
0.description: Forecast material, labor, and equipment demand across active construction projects. Optimize procurement and allocation against supplier constraints, logistics capacity, and seasonal availability.

1.title: Dynamic Construction Scheduling
1.description: Automatically adjust project schedules based on weather data, contractor availability, supply chain delays, and regulatory approvals. Recalculate critical paths in real time.

2.title: Process Optimization for Compliance Reporting
2.description: Track compliance with environmental permits, safety regulations, and contractual milestones. Generate structured reports for regulators, insurers, and financing partners with source-verified data.

3.title: Site Selection & Terrain Analysis
3.description: Integrate geospatial data, environmental impact assessments, and infrastructure connectivity models to evaluate and compare potential construction sites before committing capital.
